fields.lane: two-Mac distributed layer-sharding (master layers 0..15 on the primary 16GB M4, worker 15..30 + head on the second 16GB M4; model staged over Thunderbolt with full-file sha256 verified identical; wire over the ssh-tunneled TCP port)
fields.results: basic_v1 5-prompt pack run distributed: 2/5 (count-primes 24 tok, translate-de 16 tok) full-budget token-identical to the pinned reference; 3/5 (capital-france@6, haiku-sea@11, rust-fn@15) match a probe-verified prefix then flip at knife-edge near-ties (top-2 gaps 0.138/0.203/0.419, reference token = camelid #2 in all). Distributed output equals single-node (f32 wire)
fields.caveats: A4B MoE QAT (Q4_0 experts + Q6_K head); two-Mac distributed lane only (13.4GB memory-infeasible single-node sustained on 16GB); slow validation decode ~0.17 tok/s, not a perf claim; the 3 frontiers are the irreducible f32 near-tie class, not a bug

claim_boundary: Exact-row text-token generation within the checked basic_v1 prompt pack and API smoke envelope only. No bounded-context promotion, no performance claim beyond the raw logs, no multimodal input (fails closed with a typed error), no neighboring-row or Gemma-family support, no shared-memory claim (distributed mode is layer sharding).
